title: UnReflectAnything: RGB-Only Highlight Removal by Rendering Synthetic Specular Supervision
authors: Alberto Rota ⋅ Mert Kiray ⋅ Mert Asim Karaoglu ⋅ Patrick Ruhkamp ⋅ Elena De Momi ⋅ Nassir Navab ⋅ Benjamin Busam

sections.heading: TLDR / GIST
sections.gist: Specular highlights distort appearance, obscure texture, and hinder geometric reasoning in both natural and surgical imagery.
sections.content: Specular highlights distort appearance, obscure texture, and hinder geometric reasoning in both natural and surgical imagery. We present UnReflectAnything, an RGB-only framework that removes highlights from a single image by predicting a highlight map together with a reflection-free diffuse reconstruction. The model uses a frozen vision transformer encoder to extract multi-scale features, a lightweight head to localize specular regions, and a token-level inpainting module that restores corrupted feature patches before producing the final diffuse image. To overcome the lack of paired supervision, we introduce a Virtual Highlight Synthesis pipeline that renders physically plausible specularities using monocular geometry, Fresnel-aware shading, and randomized lighting which enables training on arbitrary RGB images with correct geometric structure. UnReflectAnything generalizes across natural and surgical domains where non-Lambertian surfaces and non-uniform lighting create severe highlights and it achieves competitive performance with state-of-the-art results on several benchmarks.

sections.heading: Problem
sections.gist: UnReflectAnything addresses a key limitation in current vision systems.
sections.content: Specular highlights distort appearance, obscure texture, and hinder geometric reasoning in both natural and surgical imagery. sections.heading: Key Insight
sections.gist: We present UnReflectAnything, an RGB-only framework that removes highlights from a single image by predicting a highlight map together with a reflection-free diffuse reconstruction.
sections.content: The paper's main contribution addresses the gap identified in the problem statement, enabling improved performance on the target task.

sections.heading: Method / Approach
sections.gist: Novel approach combining advanced vision techniques with scalable training.
